description: §17.4a / §17.2 — a plain `for` loop with no `lift` and NO `else` block does NOT fire E-CTRL-010. E-CTRL-010 is conditioned on the PRESENCE of an `else` empty-state block on a no-lift `for`; a bare `for` that never carries an `else` is an ordinary iteration statement and SHALL NOT be rejected. Twins with ctrl-010-else-on-for-without-lift-pos (same loop, `else { lift ... }` appended → fires), isolating the trigger to the `else`. W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED co-fires incidentally at severity info; not asserted.
rationale: §17.4a: 'The `else` body is valid only on a `for/lift` loop. A `for` loop without `lift` SHALL NOT accept an `else` block (E-CTRL-010).' The prohibition targets the `else` block specifically — a plain `for` loop without any `else` is fully valid (§17.4/§49). The detector fires only when an `else` bare-expr immediately follows a no-lift for-stmt; with no `else` present the condition is unmet and E-CTRL-010 SHALL stay silent.
